behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior can fail entirely inside validation/formatting logic for a merged config object; a single function can reproduce it with fixed inputs. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text focuses on error content (`names the missing key by its exact config path`), not cross-module handoff requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Call the config validation function with a config missing `database.host` and no env-derived overrides; assert the raised error string contains the exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure boundary is env reader + path resolver wiring (module handoff), not just one pure function in isolation. REAL-INFRA: still no real network/subprocess; in-process env patching is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond normal process env access, (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic flow. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is only path selection (`resolves ... relative to that directory`), not OS-level failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Patch process env with `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/customcfg`, run loader path-resolution flow, and assert the computed config path is `/tmp/customcfg/tool/config.yaml` (or project-equivalent) instead of `~/.config/...`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure appears at module boundaries (YAML load -> env mapping -> precedence merge). A unit test of only merge logic can miss bad env-key mapping or wiring. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network/subprocess beyond local process env. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior is precedence logic (`environment variable value is used`), reproducible in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Load a config file containing a key (e.g., `database.host=filehost`) while setting `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`; run full local loader pipeline and assert final config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: Mocking parser errors can hide real parser exception structure/line-number extraction bugs; use real YAML parser with malformed input. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network; local file/string parsing is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is parser/reporting (`YAML syntax error ... includes the line number`), not OS-dependent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed YAML with a known bad line, run loader with the real YAML parser, and assert the user-facing error includes that line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filesystem side effects across modules (startup check -> mkdir -> defaults write -> continue). Per level defs, local filesystem fits Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no need for subprocess/real network or special OS event semantics; temp dir repro is sufficient, (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection gate: System considered but disqualified because behavior text is basic create/write flow (`creates it and writes a defaults file`) that in-process local FS tests can reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Start with an isolated temp HOME/XDG path where config dir is absent; invoke startup loader; assert directory now exists, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader continues successfully.
